claim.statement: Every 3-coloring of {1,...,27} contains a monochromatic 3-term arithmetic progression (W(3,3) <= 27) -- the SAME claim as vdw-3-3-le-27, corroborated by a second, independent checker.
claim.negation_of: a 3-coloring of {1,...,27} with no monochromatic 3-term arithmetic progression

rung.name: independent-recomputation
trusted_base: an untrusted from-scratch Python LRAT/RUP re-implementation (tools/rup_check.py)
agrees with the R2 Lean-proved checker on the identical certificate

meta.cross_reference: same bytes as vdw-3-3-le-27 (R2); two independent checkers, two rungs

provenance.pipeline: same cert as the R2 entry; re-checked by a separate Python RUP implementation written from the Lrat.lean semantics, not the Lean code

one_directional: The Python checker fails closed (bad hint, non-unit, or no empty clause => REFUSED), and the cert is sha256+shape bound. This is R3, NOT R2: a bug in rup_check.py could in principle cause a false accept, which is exactly why it sits one rung below the kernel-proved checker. Its value is independent corroboration, not a stronger guarantee.
